response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine geospatial/GIS depth (GISCollective, Green Map) and proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and the resume shows only brief, dated C/C++ exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis), with no Rust. The bulk of recent work is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. The geospatial fit and systems-thinking are strong, but the core language requirement is a significant gap, making this a borderline maybe pending a screen on C++ ramp-up willingness.
strengths: Direct geospatial/navigation data experience via GISCollective and Green Map GIS platforms
Proven low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work (async I/O, caching, fibers) at Sociomantic
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and demonstrated willingness to learn new stacks
concerns: Minimal and dated C++ experience; no Rust at all, against a C++-primary role
Recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the required systems languages
No evidence of production routing-tile pipelines or navigation guidance with rigorous QA
